        Performance benchmarking for Code Scalpel MCP transports.
        
        [20260125_FEATURE] Phase 3: Transport optimization and performance verification
        Compares stdio (Tier 1) vs HTTP (Tier 2) transports for throughput and latency.
        
        Tests can be run with: pytest tests/benchmarks/test_transport_performance.py -v
        <Class TestTransportPerformance>
          Benchmark tests for MCP transports.
          <Function test_stdio_transport_throughput>
            Test stdio transport throughput.
            
            [20260125_FEATURE] Tier 1: stdio should have minimal overhead
            Tests raw message throughput (encode/decode cycles).
          <Function test_http_transport_throughput>
            Test HTTP transport throughput.
            
            [20260125_FEATURE] Tier 2: HTTP has network stack overhead
            Tests message throughput with HTTP encoding.
          <Function test_stdio_latency>
            Measure stdio transport latency.
            
            [20260125_FEATURE] Tier 1: stdio should have minimal latency
            Tests end-to-end latency for a single message.
          <Function test_http_latency>
            Measure HTTP transport latency.
            
            [20260125_FEATURE] Tier 2: HTTP has higher latency due to network stack
            Tests end-to-end latency with HTTP overhead.
          <Function test_transport_overhead_ratio>
            Calculate overhead ratio of HTTP vs stdio.
            
            [20260125_FEATURE] Phase 3 verification
            Confirms that stdio is faster than HTTP for local clients.

      <Package parsers>
        <Module test_base_parser.py>
          <UnitTestCase TestCodeParser>
            A class whose instances are single test cases.
            
            By default, the test code itself should be placed in a method named
            'runTest'.
            
            If the fixture may be used for many test cases, create as
            many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
            subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
            that the instance is to execute.
            
            Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
            and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
            implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
            
            If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
            __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
            should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
            of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
            in order to be run.
            
            When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
            * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
                the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
                exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
            * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
                objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
                to any explicit message passed.
            * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
                by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
                attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.
            <TestCaseFunction test_parse_cpp_code>
            <TestCaseFunction test_parse_java_code>
            <TestCaseFunction test_parse_javascript_code>
            <TestCaseFunction test_parse_python_code>
            <TestCaseFunction test_preprocess_code_normalize_whitespace>
            <TestCaseFunction test_preprocess_code_remove_comments>
          <UnitTestCase TestCodeParserAdditional>

            __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
            should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances

          <UnitTestCase TestJavaNormalizerIntegration>
            A class whose instances are single test cases.
            
            By default, the test code itself should be placed in a method named
            'runTest'.
            
            If the fixture may be used for many test cases, create as
            many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
            subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
            that the instance is to execute.
            
            Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
            and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
            implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
            
            If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
            __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
            should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
            of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
            in order to be run.
            
            When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
            * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
                the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
                exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
            * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
                objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
                to any explicit message passed.
            * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
                by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
                attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.
            <TestCaseFunction test_class_extends_implements_and_nested_with_generics>
            <TestCaseFunction test_method_and_parameter_annotations_preserved>
        <Module test_java_security_sinks.py>
          <Function test_spring_jpa_oauth_saml_sinks_present[entityManager.createNamedQuery-SecuritySink.SQL_QUERY]>
          <Function test_spring_jpa_oauth_saml_sinks_present[entityManager.createStoredProcedureQuery-SecuritySink.SQL_QUERY]>
          <Function test_spring_jpa_oauth_saml_sinks_present[Query.setParameter-SecuritySink.SQL_QUERY]>
          <Function test_spring_jpa_oauth_saml_sinks_present[TypedQuery.setParameter-SecuritySink.SQL_QUERY]>
          <Function test_spring_jpa_oauth_saml_sinks_present[JpaRepository.deleteBy-SecuritySink.SQL_QUERY]>

        <Module test_pdg_audit.py>
          PDG Tools Audit Tests
          
          Comprehensive correctness and semantic validation tests for the PDG tools.
          These tests verify that the PDG accurately represents program semantics,
          not just coverage metrics.
          
          Test Categories:
          1. Data Dependency Correctness - verifies data flow edges are accurate
          2. Control Dependency Correctness - verifies control flow edges are accurate
          3. Scope Chain Correctness - verifies variable resolution follows Python scoping
          4. Semantic Equivalence - verifies PDG preserves program meaning
          5. Edge Cases - unusual but valid Python constructs
          6. Regression Tests - specific scenarios that could break
          <Class TestDataDependencyCorrectness>
            Verify data dependencies are semantically correct.
            <Function test_simple_data_flow>
              x = 1; y = x should create edge from x_def to y_def.
            <Function test_no_spurious_data_dependency>
              Independent assignments should NOT have data dependencies.
            <Function test_chain_data_dependency>
              x = 1; y = x; z = y should create chain x -> y -> z.
            <Function test_multiple_uses_same_variable>
              x = 1; y = x + x should create single dep from x to y.
            <Function test_reassignment_breaks_chain>
              x = 1; x = 2; y = x should use second x, not first.
          <Class TestControlDependencyCorrectness>
            Verify control dependencies are semantically correct.
            <Function test_if_body_has_control_dependency>
              Statements in if body should depend on if condition.
            <Function test_else_body_has_control_dependency>
              Statements in else body should depend on if condition.
            <Function test_nested_control_dependencies>
              Nested if should have transitive control dependencies.
            <Function test_loop_body_has_control_dependency>
              Statements in loop body should depend on loop (control or loop dep).
            <Function test_outside_control_no_dependency>
              Statements outside control structures should not have ctrl deps.
          <Class TestScopeChainCorrectness>
            Verify variable scoping follows Python semantics.
            <Function test_function_scope_isolation>
              Variables in function should not leak to outer scope.
            <Function test_parameter_as_data_source>
              Function parameters should serve as data sources.
            <Function test_scope_enter_exit>
              Scope should be properly managed on enter/exit.
            <Function test_nested_scope_variable_shadowing>
              Inner scope variable should shadow outer scope.
          <Class TestEdgeCases>
            Test unusual but valid Python constructs.
            <Function test_walrus_operator>
              Named expression (walrus) := should be handled.
            <Function test_starred_assignment>
              Starred unpacking *rest should be handled.
            <Function test_multiple_assignment_targets>
              x = y = z = 1 should create multiple targets.
            <Function test_nested_function_calls>
              f(g(h(x))) should create proper call nodes.
            <Function test_comprehension_scope>
              List comprehension has its own scope in Python 3.
            <Function test_lambda_expression>
              Lambda should create a function-like construct.
            <Function test_decorator_with_arguments>
              @decorator(arg) should be handled.
            <Function test_class_decorator>
              @decorator on class should be handled.
            <Function test_async_function>
              async def should be handled like regular function.
            <Function test_try_finally>
              try/finally without except should work.
            <Function test_try_except_else>
              try/except/else should handle else block.
            <Function test_match_statement>
              Python 3.10 match/case should not crash.
            <Function test_global_statement>
              global x should be handled.
            <Function test_nonlocal_statement>
              nonlocal x should be handled.
          <Class TestAnalyzerCorrectness>
            Verify PDGAnalyzer produces correct results.
            <Function test_cyclomatic_complexity_if>
              Each if adds 1 to complexity.
            <Function test_cyclomatic_complexity_loops>
              while and for add to complexity.
            <Function test_dead_code_detection>
              Nodes with no outgoing edges (except return) are dead code.
            <Function test_undefined_variable_detection>
              Variables used but never defined should be flagged.
            <Function test_unused_variable_detection>
              Variables defined but never used should be flagged.
            <Function test_taint_source_identification>
              input() calls should be taint sources.
            <Function test_taint_sink_identification>
              eval() calls should be taint sinks.
            <Function test_sanitized_path_detection>
              Path with escape() should be marked sanitized.
            <Function test_unsanitized_path_detection>
              Path without sanitizer should NOT be marked sanitized.
          <Class TestSlicerCorrectness>
            Verify ProgramSlicer produces correct slices.
            <Function test_backward_slice_includes_dependencies>
              Backward slice should include all data dependencies.
            <Function test_backward_slice_excludes_unrelated>
              Backward slice should NOT include unrelated nodes.
            <Function test_forward_slice_includes_dependents>
              Forward slice should include all data dependents.
            <Function test_chop_computes_intersection>

            <Function test_thin_slice_no_transitive>

        <UnitTestCase TestCodeAnalyzerAdditional>
          A class whose instances are single test cases.
          
          By default, the test code itself should be placed in a method named
          'runTest'.
          
          If the fixture may be used for many test cases, create as
          many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
          subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
          that the instance is to execute.
          
          Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
          and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
          implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
          
          If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
          __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
          should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
          of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
          in order to be run.
          
          When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
          * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
              the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
              exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
          * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
              objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
              to any explicit message passed.
          * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
              by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
              attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.
          <TestCaseFunction test_generate_refactor_suggestions_handles_deep_nesting>
          <TestCaseFunction test_run_symbolic_execution_handles_invalid_code>

            <Coroutine test_spring_jpa_cross_file_sink_detected[entityManager.createNamedQuery(f"select * from users where name = {name}")]>
            <Coroutine test_spring_jpa_cross_file_sink_detected[JdbcTemplate.batchUpdate(f"delete from users where name = {name}")]>

        <Module test_adversarial_v25.py>
          Adversarial Tests for v2.5.0 Guardian Features.
          
          # [20250108_TEST] v2.5.0 Guardian - Adversarial security tests
          
          These tests verify that the v2.5.0 features resist adversarial attacks
          and edge cases identified in the 3rd party security review.
          
          Test Categories:
          - ADV-2.5.1: Telephone Game (Confidence Decay accuracy)
          - ADV-2.5.2: Threshold Rejection (Low-confidence blocking)
          - ADV-2.5.3: Context Explosion (Graph truncation)
          - ADV-2.5.4: Relevance Test (Confidence-based pruning)
          - ADV-2.5.5: chmod Bypass (Hash verification)
          - ADV-2.5.6: Manifest Tamper (HMAC signature verification)
          <Class TestADV251TelephoneGame>
            ADV-2.5.1: "Telephone Game" Test
            
            Create a 10-hop dependency chain where each link is 90% confident.
            Verify the reported confidence at the end is ~34% (0.9^10 ≈ 0.349),
            not 90%. This validates that confidence compounds correctly across
            transitive dependencies.
            <Function test_telephone_game_confidence_decay>
              ADV-2.5.1: Verify 10-hop chain shows ~34% confidence at depth 10.
              
              Formula: C_effective = C_base × 0.9^depth
              At depth 10: 1.0 × 0.9^10 = 0.3486784401 ≈ 0.349
              
              This test ensures confidence is NOT reported as 90% (single-hop)
              but rather ~34% (compound decay).
            <Function test_telephone_game_cumulative_decay>
              Verify confidence decays cumulatively at each level.
              
              Level 0: 1.0
              Level 1: 0.9
              Level 2: 0.81
              Level 3: 0.729
              ...
              Level 10: 0.349
            <Function test_telephone_game_extreme_decay_factor_zero>
              ADV-2.5.1 ADVERSARIAL: decay_factor=0 should immediately kill confidence.
              
              With decay_factor=0, ANY dependency beyond depth 0 should have
              confidence = 0. This tests the boundary condition.
            <Function test_telephone_game_extreme_decay_factor_one>
              ADV-2.5.1 ADVERSARIAL: decay_factor=1.0 should preserve full confidence.
              
              With decay_factor=1.0, confidence should NOT decay at all.
              All dependencies should have confidence = 1.0.
            <Function test_telephone_game_extreme_decay_factor_greater_than_one>
              ADV-2.5.1 ADVERSARIAL: decay_factor > 1.0 should be clamped or rejected.
              
              A malicious agent might try decay_factor=1.5 to INCREASE confidence
              at depth. The system MUST NOT allow confidence > 1.0.
              
              SECURITY FINDING: This test FAILED because confidence values exceeded 1.0.
              The calculate_confidence() function does not clamp decay_factor.
              
              REMEDIATION: Add input validation to clamp decay_factor to [0.0, 1.0]
            <Function test_telephone_game_negative_decay_factor>
              ADV-2.5.1 ADVERSARIAL: Negative decay_factor should be rejected.
              
              Negative decay creates oscillating confidence which is nonsensical.
              The system should reject or clamp to valid range.
              
              SECURITY FINDING: This test FAILED because confidence values were negative.
              The calculate_confidence() function does not validate decay_factor >= 0.
              
              REMEDIATION: Add input validation to reject negative decay_factor
            <Function test_telephone_game_float_precision_attack>
              ADV-2.5.1 ADVERSARIAL: Test float precision doesn't cause confidence overflow.
              
              With very small decay factors and deep chains, verify no underflow/overflow.
          <Class TestADV252ThresholdRejection>
            ADV-2.5.2: Threshold Rejection Test
            
            Verify that an agent is blocked from acting on a deep transitive
            dependency that falls below the min_confidence threshold.
            
            With 0.9 decay:
            - Depth 7: 0.9^7 = 0.478 (below 0.5 threshold)
            - Depth 8: 0.9^8 = 0.430
            <Function test_threshold_rejection_flags_low_confidence>
              Verify low-confidence symbols are flagged.
              
              At depth 7+ with 0.9 decay, confidence < 0.5 (default threshold).
              These should be identified as low confidence.
            <Function test_threshold_rejection_warning_generated>
              Verify warning is generated when extracting low-confidence symbols.
            <Function test_mcp_tool_reports_low_confidence>
              Verify MCP tool includes low_confidence_warning in response.
            <Function test_threshold_boundary_exact_threshold>
              ADV-2.5.2 ADVERSARIAL: Test EXACT boundary at threshold.
              
              At depth 6 with 0.9 decay: 0.9^6 = 0.531441 (just above 0.5)
              At depth 7 with 0.9 decay: 0.9^7 = 0.478297 (just below 0.5)
              
              Verify boundary behavior is EXACTLY correct.
            <Function test_threshold_attack_custom_threshold_bypass>
              ADV-2.5.2 ADVERSARIAL: Attempt to bypass by providing custom threshold.
              
              If the API allows custom thresholds, verify the system still
              uses a MINIMUM threshold that cannot be set to 0.
            <Function test_threshold_attack_negative_depth>
              ADV-2.5.2 ADVERSARIAL: Attempt extraction with negative depth.
              
              Negative depth is nonsensical. System should reject or clamp to 0.
            <Function test_threshold_attack_huge_depth>
              ADV-2.5.2 ADVERSARIAL: Attempt extraction with huge depth (DoS prevention).
              
              With depth=10000, system should not hang or consume excessive memory.
              Either cap depth or fail gracefully.
          <Class TestADV253ContextExplosion>
            ADV-2.5.3: Context Explosion Test
            
            Request a neighborhood on a dense "hub" node (e.g., common utility
            called by 500+ callers). Verify the result contains exactly max_nodes
            (default 100) and NOT the entire graph.
            <Function test_context_explosion_truncation>
              ADV-2.5.3: Verify dense hub is truncated to max_nodes.
              
              With 500 callers, requesting neighborhood with max_nodes=100
              should return exactly 100 nodes, not 501.
            <Function test_context_explosion_exactly_max_nodes>
              Verify truncated graph contains exactly max_nodes (not fewer).
            <Function test_context_explosion_different_max_nodes>
              Verify max_nodes parameter is respected with different values.
          <Class TestADV254Relevance>
            ADV-2.5.4: Relevance Test
            
            When truncation occurs, ensure the pruned nodes are the LOWEST
            confidence ones, not randomly selected. Create a graph with known
            confidence values and verify the retained nodes are the highest-
            confidence paths from center.
            <Function test_relevance_high_confidence_retained>
              ADV-2.5.4: Verify high-confidence paths are retained when truncating.
            <Function test_relevance_confidence_filtering>
              Verify min_confidence parameter filters correctly.
            <Function test_relevance_determinism>
              ADV-2.5.4 ADVERSARIAL: Verify truncation is DETERMINISTIC.
              
              Run neighborhood extraction 10 times with same parameters.
              Results MUST be identical every time (no random tie-breaking).
            <Function test_context_explosion_memory_bound>
              ADV-2.5.3 ADVERSARIAL: Verify memory is bounded.
              
              With 500 nodes and max_nodes=10, memory usage should NOT
              explode to hold the full graph.
            <Function test_context_explosion_zero_max_nodes>
              ADV-2.5.3 ADVERSARIAL: max_nodes=0 should return only center or fail.
            <Function test_context_explosion_negative_max_nodes>
              ADV-2.5.3 ADVERSARIAL: Negative max_nodes should be rejected.
            <Function test_context_explosion_negative_k>
              ADV-2.5.3 ADVERSARIAL: Negative k (radius) should be rejected.
            <Function test_context_explosion_huge_k>
              ADV-2.5.3 ADVERSARIAL: Huge k should still respect max_nodes.
            <Function test_relevance_invalid_min_confidence>
              ADV-2.5.4 ADVERSARIAL: Invalid min_confidence values.
            <Function test_relevance_tie_breaking_consistency>
              ADV-2.5.4 ADVERSARIAL: When confidences are EXACTLY equal, tie-breaking must be deterministic.
              
              Create graph with identical confidence values and verify consistent ordering.
          <Class TestADV255ChmodBypass>
            ADV-2.5.5: chmod Bypass Test
            
            Simulate an agent attack: chmod +w .code-scalpel/policy.yaml, edit the
            file to add "allow_all: true", then verify that Code Scalpel refuses
            to operate because SHA-256 hash no longer matches the signed manifest.
            <Function test_chmod_bypass_detected>
              ADV-2.5.5: Simulate agent bypassing chmod protection.
              
              1. Make file writable: chmod +w policy.yaml
              2. Modify policy: add "allow_all: true"
              3. Attempt verification -> MUST FAIL
            <Function test_chmod_bypass_hash_mismatch_details>
              Verify specific hash mismatch is reported.
          <Class TestADV256ManifestTamper>
            ADV-2.5.6: Manifest Tamper Test
            
            Edit policy.manifest.json to change the signature to match a fake
            policy. Assert that HMAC signature verification fails with
            SecurityError and all operations are DENIED (fail closed).
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_fake_signature>
              ADV-2.5.6: Replace signature with a fake one.
              
              Attack: Modify manifest signature to match a different policy.
              Result: HMAC verification must fail.
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_modified_hashes>
              Attack: Modify the file hashes in the manifest.
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_wrong_secret>
              Attack: Try to verify with a different secret key.
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_replay_old_manifest>
              Attack: Try to use an old manifest with a modified policy.
              
              Scenario:
              1. Save copy of valid manifest
              2. Update policy legitimately (new manifest created)
              3. Replace new manifest with old one
              4. Verification should fail (policy hash mismatch)
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_empty_signature>
              ADV-2.5.6 ADVERSARIAL: Empty signature should be rejected.
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_null_signature>
              ADV-2.5.6 ADVERSARIAL: Null/None signature should be rejected.
              
              Tests that the system fails closed when signature is null.
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_missing_signature_field>
              ADV-2.5.6 ADVERSARIAL: Missing signature field should fail closed at load.
              
              Tests that the system fails closed when signature field is missing.
              This is correct behavior - fail closed at load time.
            <Function test_chmod_bypass_symlink_attack>
              ADV-2.5.5 ADVERSARIAL: Symlink attack to bypass policy verification.
              
              Attack: Create symlink from policy.yaml to /etc/passwd or malicious file.
              System must verify the ACTUAL file content, not follow symlinks blindly.
            <Function test_chmod_bypass_race_condition>
              ADV-2.5.5 ADVERSARIAL: TOCTOU (Time-of-check to time-of-use) race.
              
              This test verifies the system re-verifies at use time, not just load time.
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_unicode_confusion>
              ADV-2.5.6 ADVERSARIAL: Unicode lookalike attack.
              
              Attack: Use Unicode characters that LOOK like ASCII but have different bytes.
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_json_injection>
              ADV-2.5.6 ADVERSARIAL: JSON injection via crafted field values.
            <Function test_chmod_bypass_empty_secret_key>
              ADV-2.5.5 ADVERSARIAL: Empty secret key should fail closed at load.
              
              System correctly fails closed when SCALPEL_MANIFEST_SECRET is empty.
            <Function test_manifest_tamper_missing_env_secret>
              ADV-2.5.6 ADVERSARIAL: Missing env secret should FAIL CLOSED at load.
              
              System correctly fails closed when SCALPEL_MANIFEST_SECRET is not set.

        <Module test_new_vulnerabilities.py>
          Test Suite for New Vulnerability Detection: WEAK_CRYPTO and SSRF
          
          These tests verify that the security analyzer correctly detects:
          1. Weak Cryptography (CWE-327): MD5, SHA1, DES usage with tainted data
          2. SSRF (CWE-918): Server-Side Request Forgery with user-controlled URLs

          - Create code with tainted input flowing to vulnerable sink
          - Run security analyzer
          - Verify vulnerability is detected with correct type

        <Module test_type_inference.py>
          Test Suite for Type Inference Engine - Milestone M1
          ====================================================
          
          TDD: These tests define the behavior BEFORE implementation.
          
          Scope (per RFC-001):
          - Integer literals and operations → Z3 Int
          - Boolean literals and comparisons → Z3 Bool
          - Everything else → None (unsupported)
          
          Gall's Law: Start simple. Strings, lists, objects are OUT OF SCOPE.
          <Class TestIntegerInference>
            Test cases for integer type inference.
            <Function test_integer_literal>
              x = 42 → x is Int
            <Function test_negative_integer_literal>
              x = -10 → x is Int
            <Function test_integer_addition>
              x = 1; y = x + 2 → both are Int
            <Function test_integer_arithmetic_chain>
              a = 1; b = 2; c = a + b * 3 → all Int
            <Function test_integer_modulo>
              x = 10 % 3 → x is Int
            <Function test_integer_floor_division>
              x = 10 // 3 → x is Int
          <Class TestBooleanInference>
            Test cases for boolean type inference.
            <Function test_bool_literal_true>
              x = True → x is Bool
            <Function test_bool_literal_false>
              x = False → x is Bool
            <Function test_comparison_produces_bool>
              x = 1; y = x > 10 → x is Int, y is Bool
            <Function test_equality_produces_bool>
              x = 5; y = x == 5 → y is Bool

              a = True; b = False; c = a and b → all Bool
            <Function test_boolean_or>
              x = True or False → x is Bool
            <Function test_boolean_not>
              x = True; y = not x → both Bool
            <Function test_chained_comparison>
              x = 5; y = 1 < x < 10 → y is Bool

          <Class TestFalsePositiveRate>
            Test false positive rate on clean code.
            <Function test_clean_python_code_no_false_positives>
              Clean Python code should not trigger false positives.
            <Function test_safe_parameterized_query_lower_confidence>
              Parameterized queries should have lower confidence than raw SQL.
              
              PreparedStatement has 0.9 in the registry (same as other JDBC
              statements) because static pattern matching cannot distinguish
              parameterised vs concatenated usage.  Verify it is no higher than
              raw Statement sinks.
          <Class TestUnsupportedLanguage>

            <Function test_invalid_confidence_too_high>
              Confidence > 1.0 should raise ValueError.
            <Function test_invalid_confidence_negative>
              Negative confidence should raise ValueError.

    <Package symbolic>
      <Module test_constraint_solver.py>
        Tests for ConstraintSolver - The Oracle of Symbolic Execution.
        
        CRITICAL: These tests enforce TYPE MARSHALING.
        Raw Z3 objects are useless for JSON serialization and user display.
        
        The solver must return:
        - Python int, not z3.IntNumRef
        - Python bool, not z3.BoolRef
        - Python dict, not z3.ModelRef
        
        If the Type Marshaling tests fail, the MCP server will crash
        when trying to serialize results.
        <Class TestBasicSatisfiability>
          Test basic constraint solving.
          <Function test_simple_int_constraint>
            Solve x > 10.
          <Function test_simple_bool_constraint>
            Solve flag == True.
          <Function test_multiple_constraints>
            Solve x > 10 AND x < 20.
          <Function test_multiple_variables>
            Solve x + y == 10 AND x > y.
          <Function test_mixed_int_bool>
            Solve with both int and bool variables.
        <Class TestUnsatisfiability>
          Test detection of unsatisfiable constraints.
          <Function test_simple_contradiction>
            x > 10 AND x < 5 is UNSAT.
          <Function test_boolean_contradiction>
            flag AND NOT flag is UNSAT.
          <Function test_equality_contradiction>
            x == 5 AND x == 10 is UNSAT.
          <Function test_complex_unsat>
            Complex constraint that's unsatisfiable.
        <Class TestTypeMarshaling>
          CRITICAL TESTS: These enforce Python-native return types.
          
          If the solver returns Z3 objects, JSON serialization crashes
          and the MCP server fails.
          <Function test_int_is_python_int>
            MARSHALING TEST 1: Integer results are Python int, not Z3.
          <Function test_bool_is_python_bool>
            MARSHALING TEST 2: Boolean results are Python bool, not Z3.
          <Function test_false_is_python_false>
            MARSHALING TEST 3: False is Python False, not Z3 BoolRef.
          <Function test_negative_int_is_python_int>
            MARSHALING TEST 4: Negative integers are properly converted.
          <Function test_large_int_is_python_int>
            MARSHALING TEST 5: Large integers don't overflow.
          <Function test_model_is_dict>
            MARSHALING TEST 6: Model is a Python dict, not Z3 ModelRef.
          <Function test_json_serializable>

      <Module test_loops.py>
        Tests for Loop Support - Bounded Unrolling for Symbolic Execution.
        
        CRITICAL: These tests enforce TERMINATION GUARANTEES.
        Unbounded loops are the halting problem. Symbolic loops are worse -
        they can fork infinitely until RAM is exhausted.
        
        The interpreter must:
        1. Unroll loops up to MAX_ITERATIONS
        2. Prune paths that exceed the limit
        3. NEVER hang on `while True: pass`
        
        If the Safety Check tests hang, the engine is BROKEN.
        <Class TestConcreteWhileLoops>
          Test while loops with concrete conditions.
          <Function test_simple_counter_loop>
            x = 0
            while x < 3:
                x += 1
            # x should be 3
          <Function test_zero_iteration_loop>
            Loop condition false from start - never enters.
          <Function test_single_iteration_loop>
            Loop executes exactly once.
          <Function test_loop_with_multiple_statements>
            Loop body has multiple statements.
          <Function test_nested_concrete_loops>
            Nested loops with concrete bounds.
        <Class TestInfiniteLoopPrevention>
          CRITICAL TESTS: These verify the engine TERMINATES on infinite loops.
          
          If ANY of these tests hang, the engine is BROKEN.
          These tests should complete quickly (< 1 second).
          <Function test_while_true_terminates>
            SAFETY TEST 1: while True must NOT hang.
            
            The engine should hit MAX_ITERATIONS and stop.
          <Function test_trivially_true_condition>
            SAFETY TEST 2: while 1 > 0 must NOT hang.
          <Function test_symbolic_always_true_terminates>
            SAFETY TEST 3: Symbolic condition that's always satisfiable.
            
            while x > 0 or x <= 0 is always true, must terminate.
          <Function test_max_iterations_configurable>
            MAX_ITERATIONS can be configured at init.
          <Function test_default_max_iterations>
            Default MAX_ITERATIONS is reasonable (not infinite).
        <Class TestSymbolicWhileLoops>
          Test while loops with symbolic conditions.
          <Function test_symbolic_loop_forks_at_boundary>
            Symbolic loop should fork: one path enters, one exits.
            
            x is symbolic
            while x > 0:
                x = x - 1
            
            Should produce multiple states (some entered loop, some didn't).
          <Function test_symbolic_loop_constrained>
            Symbolic loop with preconditions.

      <Module test_symbolic_state.py>
        Tests for SymbolicState - The Memory Model for Symbolic Execution.
        
        CRITICAL: These tests specifically verify FORK ISOLATION.
        The "shallow copy suicide" is the #1 killer of symbolic execution engines.
        
        If Path A and Path B share mutable state, you get:
        - Silent corruption
        - Non-deterministic results
        - Impossible-to-debug failures
        
        The fork() method MUST provide total isolation.
        <Class TestVariableStorage>
          Test basic variable operations.
          <Function test_create_int_variable>

            Can add a single path constraint.

          <Function test_infeasible_path>

        <Class TestForkIsolation>
          CRITICAL TESTS: These verify that fork() provides TOTAL ISOLATION.
          
          If ANY of these tests fail, the symbolic execution engine is BROKEN.
          Path corruption will cause silent, impossible-to-debug failures.
          <Function test_fork_creates_new_object>
            Fork returns a different object instance.

            Forked state has the same variables as parent.
          <Function test_fork_preserves_constraints>
            Forked state has the same path constraints as parent.
          <Function test_fork_variable_isolation_add_new>
            CORRUPTION TEST 1: Adding a variable to fork does NOT affect parent.
          <Function test_fork_variable_isolation_modify_existing>
            CORRUPTION TEST 2: Modifying a variable in fork does NOT affect parent.
          <Function test_fork_constraint_isolation_add_new>
            CORRUPTION TEST 3: Adding constraints to fork does NOT affect parent.
          <Function test_fork_deep_isolation_chained_forks>
            CORRUPTION TEST 4: Chained forks maintain isolation at all levels.
            
            Simulates: if (a) { if (b) { ... } }
          <Function test_fork_isolation_real_world_if_else>
            CORRUPTION TEST 5: Simulate real if/else branching.
            
            Code being analyzed:
                x = input()  # symbolic
                if x > 0:
                    y = 1
                else:
                    y = -1
            
            Both paths must be independent.

        [20260212_SKIP] Temporarily skipped - architectural model changed.
        These tests validate the OLD tool-gating model (Pro=19 tools, Enterprise=10 tools).

        To fix these tests:
        1. Update tool count expectations: All tiers should assert 22 tools
        2. Replace resolver._LIMITS_CACHE checks with config_loader cache APIs
        3. Change assertions from tool availability to capability/limit differences
        4. See docs/guides/implementing_feature_gating.md for current architecture

    <Package tier_validation>
      <Module test_tier_structure.py>
        Tier Validation Test Suite
        ==========================
        
        Scientific validation that tier limits and capabilities are correctly
        structured according to the tier philosophy:
        
        - Community: Solo devs, ≤500 file projects, performance budget <5s
        - Pro: Teams, any project size, limits match Enterprise
        - Enterprise: Same limits as Pro + 139 governance capabilities
        
        See TIER_STRATEGY_OPTIONS.md and docs/architecture/tier_limit_justification.md
        for full methodology.
        <Class TestMonotonicity>
          Verify that for every numeric limit, Community ≤ Pro ≤ Enterprise.
          <Function test_community_leq_pro>
            Community limits must not exceed corresponding Pro limits.
          <Function test_pro_leq_enterprise>
            Pro limits must not exceed corresponding Enterprise limits.
          <Function test_full_ordering_all_numeric_limits>
            Full C ≤ P ≤ E ordering for every numeric limit across all tools.
            
            Only compares limits that are explicitly set in both tiers being compared.
            Missing values (None) mean the tool uses 'enabled = true' shorthand
            for unlimited, so they're not violations.
        <Class TestProEnterpriseParity>
          Verify that Pro and Enterprise have identical numeric limits.
          
          Enterprise differentiates on CAPABILITIES (139 unique features),
          not on limit values. Any numeric limit in Enterprise must also
          appear in Pro with the same effective value.
          <Function test_pro_matches_enterprise_limits>
            For every explicit Enterprise numeric limit, Pro must match.
        <Class TestEnterpriseSupersetOfPro>
          Verify Enterprise capabilities are a strict superset of Pro.
          <Function test_enterprise_has_all_pro_capabilities>
            Every Pro capability must also exist in Enterprise.
          <Function test_enterprise_has_additional_capabilities>
            Enterprise should have capabilities that Pro doesn't.
        <Class TestCommunityCoversPersona>
          Verify Community limits are sufficient for solo developer projects.
          <Function test_scanner_covers_project[flask_app-stats0]>
            Community scanner limits must accommodate all solo dev project types.
          <Function test_scanner_covers_project[fastapi_service-stats1]>

            Community scanner limits must accommodate all solo dev project types.
          <Function test_community_call_graph_depth_practical>

          <Function test_community_cross_file_deps_useful>

        <Module test_tiers.py>
          Tier-based tests for analyze_code MCP tool using REAL license files.
          
          [20260105_FEATURE] Created to test licensing infrastructure end-to-end.
          
          These tests complement test_tiers.py by using actual JWT licenses
          from tests/licenses/ instead of mocking tier detection.
          
          Benefits over mocked tests:
          - Tests JWT signature validation
          - Tests license file loading from CODE_SCALPEL_LICENSE_PATH
          - Tests claim validation (tier, sub, iss, aud, exp, iat, jti, nbf, org, seats)
          - Tests expiration handling
          - Tests broken license rejection
          - Tests community fallback behavior
          - Tests environment variable handling
          
          Coverage: Community, Pro, Enterprise tiers + error cases

        <Module test_tier_inheritance.py>
          Test suite proving Enterprise tier contains all Pro tier features plus Enterprise features.
          
          This test suite explicitly verifies the additive tier model:
          - Community: Base features
          - Pro: All Community + Pro features
          - Enterprise: All Pro + Enterprise features
          
          These tests are critical for CTOs and Engineers to trust that upgrading tiers
          adds capabilities without removing access to lower-tier features.
          
          [20260212_TEST] Created tier inheritance verification test suite.
          <Class TestEnterpriseInheritsAllProCapabilities>
            Verify Enterprise tier includes ALL Pro tier capabilities.
            <Function test_enterprise_has_all_pro_capabilities>
              CRITICAL: Enterprise must have every capability that Pro has.
              
              This is the additive tier model guarantee for customers.

        <Module test_tiers.py>
          Tier System Tests for Cross-File Security Scan.
          
          [20260103_TEST] v3.1.0+ - Comprehensive tier enforcement testing
          
          Tests validate:
              ✅ Community tier: max_depth=5, max_modules=50 enforced
              ✅ Pro tier: unlimited depth/modules
              ✅ Enterprise tier: unlimited depth/modules
              ✅ Feature gating: Pro/Enterprise tier features properly gated
              ✅ Invalid license fallback: Falls back to Community tier
              ✅ Confidence scoring (Pro): Only available in Pro+ tiers
              ✅ Microservice hints (Enterprise): Only available in Enterprise tier
          
          License Contract:
              - Community license → Basic taint tracking with strict limits
              - Pro license → Enhanced tracking with confidence scoring
              - Enterprise license → Unlimited analysis with microservice hints
              - Invalid/expired license → Fallback to Community tier

        <Module test_enterprise_tier.py>
          Enterprise tier tests for get_file_context.
          
          Enterprise tier adds to Pro features:
          - Custom metadata extraction from .code-scalpel/metadata.yaml
          - Compliance flags detection (HIPAA, SOC2, PCI-DSS, GDPR)
          - Code owners parsing from CODEOWNERS files
          - Technical debt score estimation
          - Historical metrics (git churn, age, contributors)
          - PII redaction (email, phone, SSN patterns)
          - Secret masking (AWS keys, API keys, passwords)
          - Unlimited line context
          - RBAC-aware retrieval
          
          These tests validate that:
          1. Custom metadata is loaded when available
          2. Compliance flags are detected for sensitive operations
          3. Code owners are parsed from CODEOWNERS files
          4. Technical debt is calculated
          5. Historical metrics are available
          6. PII and secrets are properly redacted
          7. Enterprise features only appear when capabilities enabled

        <Module test_confidence_filtering.py>
          Confidence filtering tests for get_graph_neighborhood.
          
          Tests the min_confidence parameter:
          - Filters edges by confidence score (0.0 to 1.0)
          - Lower confidence = weaker relationships, may be filtered
          - Confidence values in the graph range from 0.87 to 0.95

            <Function test_edges_below_threshold_excluded>

            <Function test_edges_at_threshold_included>
              Edges with confidence == min_confidence should be included.
            <Function test_edge_filtering_affects_node_reachability>
              Filtering edges may remove nodes from reachability.

        <Module test_direction_filtering.py>
          Direction filtering tests for get_graph_neighborhood.
          
          Tests the direction parameter:
          - direction="outgoing" - only follow calls FROM center outward
          - direction="incoming" - only follow calls TO center inward
          - direction="both" - follow in both directions (default)
          
          These tests validate filtering logic (CRITICAL for pre-release).
          <Class TestDirectionOutgoing>
            Test direction='outgoing' parameter.
            <Function test_outgoing_only_includes_called_functions>

            <Function test_outgoing_k2_extends_call_chain>

            <Function test_outgoing_excludes_callers>
              Outgoing should exclude functions that call the center.
            <Function test_outgoing_edges_point_away_from_center>
              In outgoing neighborhood, edges should flow from center outward.
          <Class TestDirectionIncoming>
            Test direction='incoming' parameter.
            <Function test_incoming_only_includes_calling_functions>
              Incoming direction should only include functions that call center.
            <Function test_incoming_k2_traces_back_callers>

            <Function test_incoming_excludes_callees>
              Incoming should exclude functions called by center.
            <Function test_incoming_edges_point_toward_center>
              In incoming neighborhood, edges should flow toward center.

            <Function test_both_k1_larger_than_incoming_k1>
              Both k=1 should include more nodes than incoming k=1.
            <Function test_both_is_default>
              Default direction (no parameter) should equal direction='both'.
            <Function test_both_includes_all_edge_types>
              Both direction should include all edges.
          <Class TestDirectionWithDepth>
            Test direction parameter interaction with k parameter.
            <Function test_outgoing_k2_vs_outgoing_k1>
              Outgoing k=2 should include more nodes than outgoing k=1.
            <Function test_incoming_k2_vs_incoming_k1>

            <Function test_invalid_direction_rejected>

            <Function test_case_sensitive_direction>
              Direction parameter should be case-sensitive.
        <Module test_enterprise_features.py>
          Tests for get_graph_neighborhood Enterprise Tier Features.
          
          Tests graph query language, custom traversals, and advanced graph metrics.
          
          **Enterprise Tier Features Tested**:
          - Graph query language (query parameter support)
          - Custom graph traversals with WHERE clauses
          - Node/edge filtering with predicates
          - Path pattern matching
          - Advanced graph metrics (degree centrality, hot nodes)
          - Query result ordering and limiting
          
          **Test Organization**:
          1. TestGraphQueryLanguageBasic - Basic query parsing and execution
          2. TestQueryNodeFiltering - WHERE clause node filtering
          3. TestQueryEdgeFiltering - Edge type and confidence filtering
          4. TestQueryPathMatching - Path pattern matching
          5. TestQueryOrdering - ORDER BY and LIMIT clauses
          6. TestAdvancedGraphMetrics - Degree centrality, hot nodes
          7. TestQueryComplexCombinations - Complex multi-clause queries
          8. TestEnterpriseTierCapabilityGating - Feature gating validation
          9. TestQueryPerformance - Query execution performance
          10. TestQueryErrorHandling - Invalid query handling
          
          **Implementation Notes**:

          - Queries operate on in-memory graph structures
          - hot_nodes feature requires Enterprise tier capability

        <Module test_pro_features.py>
          Tests for get_graph_neighborhood Pro Tier Features.
          
          Tests semantic neighbor detection and logical relationship mapping.
          
          **Pro Tier Features Tested**:
          - Semantic neighbor detection (name similarity, parameter similarity, docstring similarity)
          - Logical relationship mapping (sibling functions, test pairs, helpers, same-class methods)
          - Enhanced confidence scoring for semantic relationships
          - Interaction with core k-hop extraction
          
          **Test Organization**:
          1. TestSemanticNeighborDetection - Basic semantic neighbor behavior
          2. TestSemanticNameSimilarity - Name-based semantic matching
          3. TestSemanticParameterSimilarity - Parameter-based semantic matching
          4. TestLogicalRelationshipDetection - Basic logical relationship detection
          5. TestLogicalSiblingRelationships - Same-file function relationships
          6. TestLogicalTestImplementationPairs - Test-to-implementation mappings
          7. TestLogicalHelperRelationships - Helper function detection
          8. TestLogicalSameClassMethods - Class method relationships
          9. TestProTierIntegrationWithKHop - Semantic + k-hop combined traversal
          10. TestProTierCapabilityGating - Feature gating based on tier
          
          **Implementation Notes**:
          - Pro tier features are best-effort (don't fail main query)
          - SemanticNeighborFinder requires project files on disk
          - LogicalRelationshipDetector requires parseable Python files
          - Tests use temporary project directories with sample code

        <Module test_tier_enforcement.py>
          Tier enforcement tests for get_graph_neighborhood.
          
          Tests licensing and tier-based parameter limits:
          - Community: k <= 1, max_nodes <= 20
          - Pro: k <= 5, max_nodes <= 200
          - Enterprise: unlimited k, unlimited nodes
          - Invalid licenses fall back to Community
          
          These tests validate licensing/capability gating (CRITICAL for pre-release).

        <Module test_rename.py>
          <UnitTestCase TestRename>
            A class whose instances are single test cases.
            
            By default, the test code itself should be placed in a method named
            'runTest'.
            
            If the fixture may be used for many test cases, create as
            many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
            subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
            that the instance is to execute.
            
            Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
            and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
            implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
            
            If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
            __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
            should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
            of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
            in order to be run.
            
            When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
            * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
                the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
                exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
            * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
                objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
                to any explicit message passed.
            * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
                by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
                attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_async_function>
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_class>
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_function>
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_method>
          <UnitTestCase TestRenameSameFileReferences>
            Test that rename_symbol updates references within the same file.
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_class_with_type_hints>
              Type hints should be updated when class is renamed.
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_function_with_calls>
              Function calls should be updated when function is renamed.
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_function_with_decorator>
              Decorator usage should be updated when function is renamed.
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_method_with_self_calls>
              Method calls via self should be updated when method is renamed.
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_preserves_strings_and_comments>
              String literals and comments should NOT be updated.
            <TestCaseFunction test_rename_reports_references_updated>
              PatchResult should report number of references updated.

          the underlying implementation that processes update_symbol requests.

          2. Error Handling (2 tests): syntax errors, file not found
          3. Success Rate (2 tests): normal operations, edge cases

        <Package mcp>
          <Module test_mcp_interface.py>
            MCP Tool Interface tests for validate_paths.
            
            These tests validate the MCP tool registration and that invoking the tool
            returns a PathValidationResult-compatible object with the contract-critical
            fields needed by clients.
            
            Note: This file intentionally avoids asserting on any transport-level envelope

        <Module test_invalid_license_fallback.py>
          Invalid license fallback tests for verify_policy_integrity.
          
          # [20260103_TEST] Phase 2: Invalid license fallback tests (3 tests, 2 hours estimated)
          
          Tests verify that invalid/expired/malformed licenses fail-closed:
          - Invalid JWT → fail-closed (deny operation)
          - Expired JWT → fail-closed (deny operation)
          - Malformed license → fail-closed (deny operation)
          
          Security requirement: Never bypass security on license validation failure.
          <Function test_invalid_jwt_fails_closed>
            Invalid JWT token should fail-closed (deny operation).
            
            Validates:
            - Malformed/invalid JWT token is detected
            - Operation fails with security error
            - No fallback to lower tier or permissive mode
            
            Security: This is critical for fail-closed security model.
          <Function test_expired_jwt_fails_closed>
            Expired JWT token should fail-closed (deny operation).
            
            Validates:
            - Expired JWT token is detected
            - Operation fails with security error
            - No fallback to Community tier or bypass
            
            Security: Expired licenses must not grant any access.
          <Function test_malformed_license_fails_closed>
            Malformed license (not valid base64) should fail-closed.
            
            Validates:
            - Completely malformed license string is detected
            - Operation fails with security error
            - Error message is clear about license issue
            
            Security: Invalid license format must be treated as security issue.
          <Function test_missing_license_defaults_to_community>
            Missing license should default to Community tier (most restrictive).
            
            Validates:
            - No SCALPEL_LICENSE_JWT env var
            - Operation succeeds with Community tier features only
            - No Pro/Enterprise features available
            
            This is the safe default: Community tier is always available.

        <UnitTestCase TestCodeAnalyzerAdditional>
          A class whose instances are single test cases.
          
          By default, the test code itself should be placed in a method named
          'runTest'.
          
          If the fixture may be used for many test cases, create as
          many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
          subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
          that the instance is to execute.
          
          Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
          and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
          implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
          
          If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
          __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
          should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
          of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
          in order to be run.
          
          When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
          * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
              the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
              exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
          * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
              objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
              to any explicit message passed.
          * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
              by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
              attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.
          <TestCaseFunction test_generate_refactor_suggestions_handles_deep_nesting>
          <TestCaseFunction test_run_symbolic_execution_handles_invalid_code>
